rows.v: Whole Milk (3.25% fat), Reduced-Fat (2%), Skim (0%), Lactose-Free, Buttermilk
Whole: sweet, round, full coating; Skim: watery, clean; Buttermilk: tangy, thick, slightly acidic
Whole milk → off-dry Riesling, light Champagne; Buttermilk → crisp Albariño, Grüner Veltliner
Yogurt, Greek Yogurt, Kefir, Sour Cream
Greek yogurt: thick, tangy, high protein; Kefir: thin, effervescent, probiotic-rich; Sour cream: lush, mild acid
Greek yogurt → dry Riesling, Assyrtiko; Kefir → natural wines; Sour cream (with blini) → Champagne, vodka
Heavy Cream (36%+ fat), Butter, Ghee (clarified butter, milk solids removed)
Heavy cream: rich, coats everything; Butter: sweet-dairy, salt-forward when cultured; Ghee: nutty, pure fat, high smoke point
Butter sauces → white Burgundy, Meursault; Cream-based pasta → aged Chardonnay; Ghee dishes → Gewurztraminer
Mozzarella, Ricotta, Burrata, Cottage Cheese, Mascarpone, Cream Cheese, Chèvre
Mild, milky, high moisture; little to no rind; meant to be eaten fresh; delicate and clean flavors
Mozzarella/Burrata → Pinot Grigio, Vermentino; Chèvre → Sancerre, Sauvignon Blanc; Ricotta → Prosecco, light Chardonnay
Havarti, Muenster, Taleggio, Provolone, Gouda (young), Gruyère (young), Cheddar (mild)
Buttery to earthy; sliceable; some develop washed rinds (Taleggio: funky, fruity); aging deepens complexity
Havarti/Muenster → Pinot Blanc, light Merlot; Taleggio → Barbera, Nebbiolo; Gouda (young) → Viognier, Chardonnay
Parmigiano-Reggiano, Manchego, Aged Cheddar, Aged Gouda, Emmental, Comté, Pecorino
Crystalline texture from tyrosine crystals; concentrated, nutty, umami-rich; long finish; grating or snacking
Parmigiano → Barolo, Amarone; Manchego → Tempranillo, Rioja; Aged Cheddar → Cabernet Sauvignon, Port; Aged Gouda → Zinfandel, Tawny Port
Soft-ripened: Brie, Camembert | Blue: Roquefort (sheep), Gorgonzola (Italian), Stilton (English)
Brie/Camembert: bloomy white rind, buttery, mushroomy paste; Blues: veined with Penicillium, sharp, salty, pungent
Brie/Camembert → Champagne, Crémant, light Pinot Noir; Roquefort → Sauternes (classic); Gorgonzola → Barolo; Stilton → Vintage Port
